rationale: The citing paper uses Lambrecht and Tucker (2013) repeatedly to frame its retargeting argument, not just as a background list citation. In the literature review, it cites that retargeting is often ineffective unless consumers have well-defined preferences, and this directly motivates its expectation that price-incentive retargeting should perform better. In the results and conclusion sections, the authors explicitly interpret their own retargeting findings as consistent with Lambrecht and Tucker, showing the cited study informs both hypothesis framing and interpretation.
relation_description: Bruce, Murthi, and Rao build their retargeting framing on Lambrecht and Tucker's condition that retargeting works when preferences are more defined, then test related implications in a broader dynamic model that also includes format, message theme, and carry-over. They use the cited paper as prior empirical grounding for why and when retargeted ads should be effective.
